job_name: test all crates in the workspace with all features (ubuntu-latest)
notes: trybuild-based compile-fail test `compile_fail_full` mismatches: the expected rustc diagnostics for tests/fail/macros_type_mismatch.rs do not match the actual rustc output, indicating rustc diagnostics drifted and the expected stderr needs to be refreshed with TRYBUILD=overwrite.
root_cause_summary: The trybuild-based compile-fail test `tests-build::macros compile_fail_full` fails because the expected rustc diagnostics for `tests/fail/macros_type_mismatch.rs` no longer match the actual rustc output. Rustc's error messages have drifted (additional `help:` suggestions such as `consider using Result::expect`). Fix: regenerate the expected .stderr with `TRYBUILD=overwrite cargo test` and commit the refreshed expectations.

notes: Jest reports `No tests found, exiting with code 1` for every example under examples/with-jest*. Root cause is upstream: git refuses to operate on /work with `fatal: detected dubious ownership`, so jest can't resolve the working tree properly. Many symptom lines, single upstream cause.
root_cause_summary: The runner container's checked-out workspace at /work is owned by a different UID than the process that invokes git, so git refuses to operate with `fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/work'`. The test harness falls back to running jest against an empty view of the repository, which reports `No tests found, exiting with code 1` for every example. The surface symptom is jest failing; the fix is upstream in the runner setup: add `git config --global --add safe.directory /work` (or equivalent) before running tests.

notes: Prettier `yarn prettier-check` reports files whose formatting is out of date. Small, focused failure: the fix is to run `yarn prettier-all-fix` and commit the result.
root_cause_summary: `yarn prettier-check` (the React repo's Run prettier step) reports one file whose formatting does not match the project's Prettier configuration: packages/react-dom/src/__tests__/ReactDOMFragmentRefs-test.js. The step exits non-zero. Fix: run `yarn prettier-all` locally and commit the reformatted file.

job_name: Docstring validation, typing, and other manual pre-commit hooks
notes: pre-commit mypy (stubtest) hook reports 279 type errors across 9 files, dominated by `Module has no attribute "X" [attr-defined]` against pyarrow.compute. All errors appear in a single dense block; no other hooks fail.
root_cause_summary: The pre-commit `mypy (stubtest)` hook reports 279 type errors across 9 pandas source files. The overwhelming majority are `Module has no attribute "X" [attr-defined]` from pyarrow.compute usages; pyarrow's type stubs do not declare the compute functions pandas calls. A handful are `[no-untyped-call]` or `[assignment]`. Fix is on pandas' side (explicit annotations or `# type: ignore[attr-defined]`) or upstream in pyarrow stubs.

notes: pytest collection failure: np.datetime64('NaT') raises DeprecationWarning which pytest promotes to error. The same collection error is repeated across many test files, producing many ERROR lines with tracebacks.
root_cause_summary: pytest fails collection and many runtime tests because NumPy (nightly) now raises DeprecationWarning when constructing np.datetime64 values with the 'generic' timedelta unit, and pandas' warning-as-error configuration turns this into test failures. The fix belongs on pandas' side: update affected tests/implementations to use a specific timedelta unit, or adjust the warning filter.
